badges.label: The Whale
badges.flavor: Goes big and cashes — the house pays this one.
badges.description: Best win rate on All-In picks this season, and a winning one — above 50% on at least 3 All-Ins. Nobody earns it in a season where the best All-In record is losing.

badges.label: The Ghost
badges.flavor: Showed up for the group chat. Not the picks.
badges.description: Missed the most picks this season.

badges.label: The Grinder
badges.flavor: Can't miss a game. Every slate, every week.
badges.description: Missed nothing all season — every slate, every week. Anyone with a perfect attendance record earns it. Only awarded in seasons where attendance was recorded.

badges.label: The Choker
badges.flavor: Went all in… and all in went wrong.
badges.description: Went all in at least 3 times and lost every one of them. Nobody earns it in a season where every All-In player won at least once.

badges.label: Perfect Week
badges.flavor: Not a single wrong pick all week.
badges.description: Had at least one week with every pick correct and none missed.

badges.label: Lone Wolf
badges.flavor: Runs with no one. Fades the whole flock.
badges.description: Takes the minority side at least 5 points more often than the league average this season, further than anyone else (minimum picks required). Measured against this league in this season, not a fixed number.

badges.label: Chalk Eater
badges.flavor: Never met a favorite they didn't back.
badges.description: Backs favorites at least 15 points more than the league average this season, further than anyone else (minimum picks required). Measured against this league in this season, not an even split.

badges.label: The Comeback
badges.flavor: Started in the basement. Finished somewhere else entirely.
badges.description: Climbed further from their season low point to their final rank than anyone else.

badges.label: Best of the Rest
badges.flavor: Buried in the standings. Unbeatable for a week.
badges.description: Posted a week's single highest score at least once while sitting in the bottom half of the standings.

player.prose: Wire-to-wire at the top and you made the rest of Sunday Bets stare up at 106 points like it was rent they couldn’t pay. A 33-12-2 record is filthy enough, but the real insult was that 7-1-1 All-In mark — when you shoved your chips in, the league got the message loud and clear 💰. Week 5’s 18-point heater and an 8-win streak were the kind of stretch that breaks people, and even your “bad” week was just -1 before you got right back to stepping on throats. Oh, and a Perfect Week badge? That’s not a season, that’s a damn hostile takeover.

league.prose: Seventeen damn points was the gap, which means Beth didn’t just win Sunday Bets — she put her foot on the league’s neck for 11 weeks at the top and finished the job at 106. test2 made it interesting with a 13-win heater and all the Whale/Comeback glory, but 89 still means you spent the whole season staring up at first place 👑. And let’s pour one out for test1, who managed the cleanest faceplant in the league: from preseason king of the hill to 4th, down 3 spots and nowhere near the money. Meanwhile Mike double-dipped the humiliation with the wooden spoon at -134 plus Lone Wolf and Chalk Eater — that’s not a season, that’s getting cooked in surround sound.

recaps.prose: And that’s the regular season curtain call: Beth sat on the throne at 106 points, 17 clear of test2’s 89, and did it with a nasty 3-win heater plus a contrarian hit that only 33.33% of you had the guts to ride. test3 grabbed the week at 8 points and snatched 3rd from test1, who pulled off the classic galaxy-brain fade and slid from 3 to 4 like a damn amateur. Marcus at least landed the All-In, but Mike detonated his and finished the week at -19 on the way to a truly revolting -134 season total — got cooked, buried, and left there 💀. Meanwhile, test2 can yap all he wants, but the all-time sheet still says Beth leads him 56-46-3.

test3 kicked the door off the hinges this week: perfect card, 19 points, and the All-In cash — that’s how you make third place look a hell of a lot less permanent. Meanwhile Mike got cooked into a fine paste at -23 and somehow paired it with a busted All-In, which is elite-level disasterball 💀. Beth still sits on the throne at 106 with a 3-win heater and a 15-point cushion over test2’s 89, but don’t get too smug — even the queen managed a backfired fade this week. And test2, your all-time edge over test3 is gone too: it’s 52-49 for test3 now, so maybe keep the chirping to a minimum.

test2 kicked the door in this week: perfect card, 19 points, nailed the All-In, and vaulted from 3rd to 2nd while test1 got shoved the other way after a fade-the-crowd special blew up in his face. Beth’s still sitting on the throne at 106 with a 3-win heater, but that cushion is down to 21 over test2’s 89, so the leader can hear footsteps now 👑. Marcus deserves a little sicko respect for cashing a 33.33% contrarian hit, while test3 managed the exact opposite of clutch with a busted All-In, and Mike somehow bottomed out at -17 on the week and -134 on the season — that’s not a cold streak, that’s a damn hostage situation.

Mike dropped a 14-point week and somehow still sits at -134 on the season — that’s the kind of heater that feels like finding twenty bucks in a burning house 💀. The real knife twist was test2 going perfect, leapfrogging test3 from 4th to 3rd while test3 face-planted to a -2 week; in their little blood feud, it’s now 49-52 all-time and test3 just handed over more ground. And while Beth’s fade blew up this week, the queen of this circus still cashed the All-In, stretched the streak to 3, and keeps a fat 17-point lead at 106 — the rest of you are chasing headlights.

test2 grabbed the weekly crown with 9 points, but Beth’s the one still running this circus — 106 on the season, a 3-win heater, and a comfy 21-point lead while the rest of you fight over scraps. Marcus got absolutely cooked: week-worst -11 and an All-In faceplant, which is impressive in the worst possible way, while Mike joined him in the busted-All-In clown car. And test1, congrats on the 40% contrarian hit, but following it up with a backfired fade is how you stay 45 points off the throne instead of actually threatening it.
